wikidata_candidates.label: Herbert Robbins
wikidata_candidates.reasoning: The Wikidata entity describes a world-renowned theoretical mathematician and statistician known for work in probability and topology, whereas the index card describes an electrical engineer focused on industrial control systems and pneumatic devices at E W Bliss Company. Their career trajectories and professional fields are entirely distinct.

results.possibleIdentity: Herbert Robbins (specifically Herbert C. Robbins, 1926–2015) was a professional electrical and computer engineer active in the late 1960s and early 1970s. He was employed by the E. W. Bliss Company, a firm known for its industrial machinery and control systems, which maintained a facility in Drexel Hill, Pennsylvania (located adjacent to Media, PA). Robbins specialized in electrical engineering, control systems, electro-mechanical devices, and pneumatic devices. He was a member of Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.), a nonprofit organization established in 1966 by Billy Klüver and Robert Rauschenberg to facilitate collaborations between artists and technical professionals. His involvement with E.A.T. typically included offering technical expertise in control systems and mechanics for experimental art projects. He is distinct from the well-known Columbia University mathematician Herbert Robbins (1915–2001), though they were active during the same era.
